label: Supply
blurb: Factory→market flows across STG's production footprint, the OneProcess SAP rollout (EU live, US late 2026), and a live freight-cost signal on the main inbound lanes.

markers.label: US — SAP S/4HANA go-live late 2026
markers.detail: OneProcess replaces 12 inherited ERPs with one global SAP instance. The US deployment (late 2026) is the next go-live — and EU go-lives already caused Q3 2025 supply disruption + market-share loss. The pulsing landmine on this lens.

markers.label: Dominican Republic — Santiago / Tamboril
markers.detail: Largest production hub: handmade + machine-rolled cigars and wrapper/binder leaf. Ships to the US — the biggest inbound lane. (San Pedro site closed 2025.)

markers.label: Honduras — Danlí
markers.detail: Handmade premium cigars for the US market.

markers.label: Nicaragua — Estelí
markers.detail: Handmade premium cigars for the US market.

markers.label: DR / HN / NI → US — handmade lane
markers.detail: The main inbound ocean lane carrying handmade cigars to the US. The live freight-COST driver (Brent crude, via FRED) is in the strip above; *the per-lane Freightos FBX rate itself stays illustrative (needs a paid feed).

markers.label: Belgium — Lummen / Westerlo
markers.detail: Machine-rolled cigars for the EU. EU SAP go-lives are complete — and were the source of the Q3 2025 disruption now behind the group.

markers.label: Italy — Lucca
markers.detail: Machine-rolled cigars — Moderno Opificio del Sigaro Italiano (85%-owned, acquired 2021), an EU production site on the AR2025 footprint.

markers.label: Denmark — Assens / Svendborg / Holstebro
markers.detail: Pipe tobacco (Assens), fine-cut (Holstebro), and the owned nicotine-pouch factory (Svendborg) — the supply base behind the XQS pouch push.

markers.label: Sri Lanka — Biyagama
markers.detail: Wrapper/binder leaf feeding the cigar factories — an upstream node in the flow.

markers.label: Indonesia — Semarang / Pasuruan
markers.detail: Machine-rolled cigars plus wrapper/binder leaf — both a production and a leaf-sourcing node.

agentNote: Production geography + the OneProcess SAP timeline (EU live, US late 2026; ~DKK 130m of 2025 special items) are from STG disclosures (AR2025, Q1'26). The freight-cost driver (Brent crude) is now live via FRED; per-lane Freightos FBX rates + OTIF stay illustrative* (the per-lane rate needs a paid FBX feed; OTIF / days-of-supply need STG data).
